chunks.text: สั่งอะไรดีคะ
By the end of this video,
you'll be able to order food,
tell them how spicy you want it,
and get the bill — all in Thai.
When I first moved to Bangkok,
there was this noodle stall right next to
the same aunty would look at me and shout
สั่งอะไรดีคะ
and I would just stand there.
I had no idea what she was saying.
I would just point at the picture on the
I honestly don't know what I got.
สั่งอะไรดีคะ
it means "What would you like to order?"
And it's one of the first things you'll
hear at any food stall in Thailand.
Today I'm going to teach you eight phrases
that will get you through any meal in
I'll explain what it means, how to use it,
and when you'd actually say it.
Then I'll put them all together into a
you can hear how they sound at natural
we'll break it down sentence by sentence,
and then you'll get to practise.
OK, let's start with the vocabulary.
I'm going to go through each of the eight
For each one, I'll say the Thai,
and show you how to use it in a sentence.

Imagine you're at a street food stall in
you sit down, you order, you eat, you pay.

the way a Thai person would actually talk.
Don't try to catch every word.
see how much you can understand. Ready?
